scope.note: Public exact anchors are only published where official text or official machine-readable source material is public. Planned controls with no public fixture yet are allowed into the pilot so framework expansion can stay honest without pretending implementation already exists. Proprietary standards remain explicitly blocked until licensed framework review is complete.

controls.title: Scoped administrative MFA enforcement
controls.decompositionNote: The corridor proves narrow administrative MFA enforcement, not full identity governance or all access-review obligations.

reviews.rationale: The corridor proves that scoped administrative identities require MFA in the declared environment. That is a narrow technical subset of the broader privileged-user MFA requirement.

Scoped administrative MFA contributes to the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience of processing systems and services, but it is only one narrow technical slice of Article 32.

Scoped administrative MFA enforcement is a narrow machine-checkable slice of the Cyber Essentials user access control theme, specifically the current MFA requirement for internet-exposed services and admin access.

authoritativeSource.documentId: Cyber Essentials Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.2

reviews.rationale: The corridor proves one narrow access-control invariant that supports CAF Principle B2 on identity and access control without claiming the whole principle is satisfied.

authoritativeSource.documentId: Cyber Assessment Framework v4.0 supplementary information
authoritativeSource.locator: Principle B2 Identity and Access Control

reviews.rationale: Scoped administrative MFA enforcement is a narrow implementation slice of the identities-and-credentials management outcome under CSF 2.0.
The corridor proves a narrow organizational-user MFA condition that is safely modeled as a subset of the broader identification and authentication control.

reviews.blocker: Exact ISO 27001 anchor publication requires licensed source review. Public ISO material is sufficient to establish the clause-versus-Annex-A structure, but not a defensible open exact-anchor corpus.
reviews.rationale: The current public boundary is intentionally honest about ISO exact-anchor incompleteness.

authoritativeSource.locator: Clauses 4-10 and Annex A structural boundary

reviews.blocker: Exact SOC 2 anchor publication requires licensed or account-gated Trust Services Criteria review. Public AICPA material establishes the TSC category structure, but not a publishable exact-anchor corpus for this project.
reviews.rationale: The pilot encodes the blocker explicitly so the public mapping is not overstated.

authoritativeSource.documentId: 2017 Trust Services Criteria (With Revised Points of Focus - 2022)
authoritativeSource.locator: Trust Services Criteria category structure only

controls.title: Scoped audit logging enabled
controls.decompositionNote: The corridor proves that scoped audit logging is enabled and that a retention window is declared, but it does not yet prove centralized monitoring adequacy or a standards-specific retention threshold.

reviews.rationale: The corridor's scoped logging predicate is a narrow slice of the requirement that a centralised event logging facility be implemented.

reviews.nextAction: Strengthen the corridor from retention-window-declared to standards-threshold-checked before promoting this anchor to reviewed.
reviews.rationale: The corridor currently declares a retention window but does not yet prove the ISM's stated minimum retention duration.
The corridor's typed logging predicate is a narrow machine-checkable slice of CAF security monitoring rather than the whole monitoring objective.

authoritativeSource.documentId: Cyber Assessment Framework v4.0 supplementary information

reviews.rationale: The corridor proves a narrow protective-technology logging state that aligns with the CSF 2.0 subcategory for generating and making log records available.
The corridor's enabled-audit-logging state is a narrow subset of the broader event-logging control, while remaining explicit about retention-threshold incompleteness.

reviews.blocker: Exact ISO 27001 logging anchor publication requires licensed source review.
reviews.rationale: Public ISO material does not provide a safe exact-anchor basis for the logging corridor.

reviews.blocker: Exact SOC 2 logging anchor publication requires licensed or account-gated TSC review.
reviews.rationale: The public AICPA source establishes the existence of the TSC corpus and categories, but not a publishable exact logging crosswalk.

authoritativeSource.documentId: 2017 Trust Services Criteria (With Revised Points of Focus - 2022)

controls.title: TLS ingress hardened
controls.decompositionNote: The corridor proves HTTPS-only ingress, managed certificates, and TLS 1.2 or higher for the scoped public edge. It does not prove the whole cryptographic architecture.

reviews.rationale: The corridor's enforced HTTPS ingress is a narrow slice of the broader requirement to use encryption when data is communicated over network infrastructure.
The corridor's TLS 1.2-or-higher boundary is aligned with the public ISM TLS guidance at the connection level, but does not yet capture the whole cipher-suite surface.
Scoped TLS ingress hardening is a narrow technical slice of encryption-related measures under Article 32.
The corridor's HTTPS-only TLS boundary is a narrow implementation slice of protecting data in transit under CSF 2.0.
The corridor proves a narrow transmission confidentiality and integrity boundary for the scoped public edge, without claiming full cryptographic architecture coverage.

reviews.blocker: Exact ISO 27001 network-security anchors require licensed source review.
reviews.rationale: The public corridor is not claiming a publishable ISO exact network-security mapping yet.

reviews.blocker: Exact SOC 2 network-security anchors require licensed or account-gated TSC review.
reviews.rationale: The current public corpus does not yet publish exact SOC 2 network-security anchors.

authoritativeSource.documentId: 2017 Trust Services Criteria (With Revised Points of Focus - 2022)

controls.title: Managed ingress boundary attached
controls.decompositionNote: The corridor proves that the declared public ingress path traverses the managed default-deny boundary. It does not prove every possible route through the wider estate.

reviews.rationale: The corridor proves a narrow machine-checkable fact that the declared ingress path traverses the managed firewall boundary, which is one subset of the broader firewalls theme.

authoritativeSource.documentId: Cyber Essentials Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.2

reviews.rationale: The corridor proves a narrow boundary-protection topology fact: the scoped ingress path traverses the managed boundary rather than bypassing it.

reviews.blocker: Exact ISO 27001 ingress-boundary anchors require licensed source review.
reviews.rationale: The control stays explicit that ISO network-security exact anchors are still blocked.

reviews.blocker: Exact SOC 2 ingress-boundary anchors require licensed or account-gated TSC review.
reviews.rationale: The control keeps the SOC 2 blocker explicit instead of publishing guessed network-topology anchors.

authoritativeSource.documentId: 2017 Trust Services Criteria (With Revised Points of Focus - 2022)

controls.title: Administrative ingress restricted
controls.decompositionNote: The corridor proves that administrative ingress is restricted to declared approved source ranges at the scoped managed boundary. It does not prove the adequacy of the allowlist itself.

reviews.rationale: Approved-source restriction for administrative ingress is a narrow machine-checkable slice of the broader firewalls control theme.

authoritativeSource.documentId: Cyber Essentials Requirements for IT Infrastructure v3.2

reviews.rationale: The corridor proves a narrow approved-source boundary-protection invariant for administrative ingress, not the whole boundary-protection architecture.

reviews.blocker: Exact ISO 27001 approved-source boundary anchors require licensed source review.
reviews.rationale: The public corpus still records ISO network exact-anchor blockers explicitly.

reviews.blocker: Exact SOC 2 approved-source boundary anchors require licensed or account-gated TSC review.
reviews.rationale: The public review layer stays explicit that SOC 2 network exact anchors are still blocked.

controls.title: Plaintext transport disabled
controls.decompositionNote: The corridor proves that the scoped public ingress path disables plaintext transport. It does not prove downstream service-to-service cryptographic posture.

reviews.rationale: Disabling plaintext transport on the scoped public ingress path is a narrow subset of the broader requirement to encrypt communications over network infrastructure.
Disabling plaintext transport is a narrow technical slice of encryption-related measures under Article 32.
The corridor's plaintext-disabled predicate is a narrow implementation slice of protecting data in transit under CSF 2.0.
The corridor proves a narrow transmission-confidentiality boundary for the scoped public edge by disabling plaintext transport.

reviews.blocker: Exact ISO 27001 plaintext-transport anchors require licensed source review.
reviews.rationale: The public corpus still blocks ISO exact-anchor publication for network-security and cryptographic text.

reviews.blocker: Exact SOC 2 plaintext-transport anchors require licensed or account-gated TSC review.
reviews.rationale: The public review layer remains honest that SOC 2 transport-security anchors are not yet publishable.

authoritativeSource.documentId: 2017 Trust Services Criteria (With Revised Points of Focus - 2022)

controls.title: Encryption at rest enabled
controls.decompositionNote: The corridor proves that encryption at rest is enabled for the scoped customer data stores and that no unencrypted store is present in the declared slice. It does not prove full cryptographic key management or every storage location outside the declared scope.

reviews.rationale: The corridor proves that the scoped customer data stores are encrypted at rest and that no unencrypted store remains in scope. That is a narrow functional subset of the ISM requirement to implement full disk or appropriately constrained partition encryption when encrypting data at rest.
Scoped encryption at rest is a narrow technical slice of the pseudonymisation-and-encryption measures named in Article 32(1)(a). It does not exhaust the wider security-of-processing obligation.

reviews.nextAction: Confirm the exact CSF 2.0 subcategory identifier against the reference tool before promoting this anchor from candidate to reviewed.
reviews.rationale: The corridor clearly targets the data-at-rest protection outcome, but the exact CSF 2.0 identifier should be rechecked directly against the current reference tool before promotion.
The corridor proves a narrow subset of protection-of-information-at-rest focused on the declared customer data stores and explicit absence of unencrypted storage in that slice.

reviews.blocker: Exact ISO 27001 encryption-at-rest anchors require licensed source review before public exact-anchor publication.
reviews.rationale: The public corpus should not guess at clause or Annex A cryptography anchors from structural hints alone.

reviews.blocker: Exact SOC 2 encryption-at-rest anchors require licensed or account-gated Trust Services Criteria review before public publication.
reviews.rationale: The public review layer stays honest by recording the blocker rather than publishing guessed encryption-at-rest criteria or points of focus.

authoritativeSource.documentId: 2017 Trust Services Criteria (With Revised Points of Focus - 2022)

controls.title: Backup schedule declared
controls.decompositionNote: The corridor proves that snapshots are enabled with a declared schedule and immutable window. It does not yet prove a full recovery program or restoration success.

reviews.rationale: The corridor's snapshot schedule is a narrow machine-checkable slice of the broader requirement to perform and retain backups in line with continuity requirements.
The corridor's immutable-window boundary is a narrow technical slice of retaining backups in a secure and resilient manner.
The corridor's scheduled immutable snapshots are a narrow machine-checkable slice of the CSF 2.0 backup-preparation outcome.
The corridor proves a narrow scheduled-backup boundary that aligns with the system-backup control while remaining explicit that restore success is out of scope.

reviews.blocker: Exact ISO 27001 backup anchors require licensed source review.
reviews.rationale: The public backup corridor is not yet expressed as a reviewed ISO exact-anchor set.

reviews.blocker: Exact SOC 2 backup and recovery anchors require licensed or account-gated TSC review.
reviews.rationale: The project is not yet claiming a publishable exact SOC 2 backup crosswalk.

controls.title: Restore exercise evidence present
controls.decompositionNote: The corridor only proves that restore-exercise evidence exists. It does not prove that every restoration path succeeds.

reviews.rationale: A restore-test attestation is a narrow documentary slice of the broader requirement to test restoration as part of disaster recovery exercises.

reviews.blocker: Exact ISO 27001 recovery-test anchors require licensed source review.

controls.title: Privacy by design adequacy review boundary
controls.decompositionNote: This control is intentionally judgment-routed. The pilot records an exact public source anchor without pretending the requirement is mechanically decidable.

reviews.rationale: The control is an explicit adequacy-review boundary for privacy by design and default, not a theorem. The exact anchor is public, but the route remains judgment rather than proof.

controls.title: Processor register current
controls.decompositionNote: The current corridor only proves that a signed attestation states the processor register is current and DPA coverage is declared for the review window.

reviews.rationale: The corridor's attested DPA-coverage statement is a narrow documentary slice of processor-contract obligations under Article 28 without claiming the whole processor-management program is complete.

controls.title: Default-deny network boundary in place
controls.decompositionNote: This planned corridor would prove a narrow boundary-protection invariant from typed firewall or security-group exports without claiming whole-architecture adequacy.

reviews.rationale: A default-deny machine-readable boundary state is a narrow machine-checkable slice of the Cyber Essentials firewalls control theme.

controls.title: AI system context and intended use documented
controls.decompositionNote: This planned corridor would only prove that a signed attestation describes intended use, deployment context, owner, and affected stakeholder class for the scoped AI system.

reviews.rationale: The planned context attestation is a narrow documentary slice of the AI RMF outcome that mission, goals, and context for AI technology are understood and documented.

reviews.nextAction: Strengthen the planned corridor from a signed context attestation to a typed technical-documentation package before promoting this AI Act anchor.
reviews.rationale: The planned context control clearly overlaps with technical documentation and intended-purpose requirements, but the current attestation boundary is weaker than the full AI Act documentation surface.

reviews.nextAction: Confirm the candidate clause set against licensed ISO/IEC 42001 text before promoting it beyond public-crosswalk status.
reviews.rationale: Public NIST crosswalk material strongly suggests these ISO/IEC 42001 clauses are relevant, but exact publication still needs licensed-source confirmation.

reviews.nextAction: Confirm the candidate ISO/IEC 42005 clause set against the published standard before promoting it beyond crosswalk-derived status.
reviews.rationale: Public AI impact-assessment crosswalk material indicates overlap with documenting AI-system context and intended use, but the exact clause-grade mapping needs licensed review.
